creator: ade@FreeBSD.org
text:
Responsible Changed
From-To: portmgr->ade

Your reasoning is fundamentally flawed given the vast majority of ports 
that have implicit dependencies on shlib versions, but do not register 
them directly in the makefile. 

For example, port A (which uses functions from gettext), but depends on 
port B (which has a direct dependency on gettext), for the most part, 
does not indicate that it itself needs gettext. 

Therefore, your spammage of 5k+ ports that have obvious direct dependencies 
will lure folks into a false sense of security, since there are considerably 
more ports out that that depend on gettext in an indirect manner. 

Like it or not, portupgrade and/or portmaster are the de-facto upgrade 
paths used by our end-users.  The ports/UPDATING entry supplied as part 
of this upgrade will absolutely ensure that things are recompiled to 
a known-state (hint: I already did this with a workstation with 2k+ ports 
installed). 

Bumping PORTREVISION is entirely reasonable, assuming it is done for 
*every* *single* *port* that depends on gettext. 

The patch, as is, does not do that, and will thus cause far more confusion 
than it resolves.  This is a bad thing.
